acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
With reference to the Modernization of Command Area Development and Water Management (M-CADWM), consider the following statements:
1. It is a sub-scheme of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) approved for the period 2025-2026.
2. It aims to establish underground pressurized piped irrigation systems up to the farm gate for landholdings up to 1 hectare.
3. It incorporates Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and Internet of Things (IoT) technology for water accounting.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Union Cabinet approved M-CADWM as a sub-scheme of PMKSY for 2025-2026. Statement 2 is correct: The scheme focuses on building robust backend infrastructure for micro-irrigation using underground pressurized piped irrigation up to the farm gate for holdings up to 1 hectare. Statement 3 is correct: It specifically mentions the use of SCADA and IoT technologies for water accounting and management to increase water use efficiency.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: M-CADWM integrates modern technology (SCADA/IoT) and pressurized piping to modernize traditional command area irrigation.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Union Cabinet approved Modernization Of Command Area Development and Water Management (M-CADWM) as a sub-scheme of Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) for the period 2025-2026... It will make robust backend infrastructure for micro-irrigation by farmers from established source to the Farm gate up to 1 hectare with underground pressurized piped irrigation... Use of Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA), Internet of things technology will be used for water accounting and water management.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Strong multi-statement question covering the technical and administrative aspects of the new M-CADWM scheme.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt: In the context of the Modernization of Command Area Development and Water Management (M-CADWM), what does 'Irrigation Management Transfer' (IMT) primarily entail?

options.text: The transfer of ownership of major canal networks from State Governments to the Central Government.
The shifting of management responsibilities for irrigation assets to Water User Societies (WUS) to ensure sustainable farming.
The transition from traditional flood irrigation to commercial drip irrigation projects managed by private technology firms.
The handover of water tax collection duties from the Revenue Department to Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).

acceptedQuestions.explanation: Irrigation Management Transfer (IMT) refers to the transfer of management responsibilities for irrigation assets to Water User Societies (WUS). This is a key feature of the M-CADWM scheme aimed at promoting sustainable farming through local participation. These societies also receive handholding support for five years to help them connect with economic entities like Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: IMT is a decentralization strategy in irrigation management aimed at empowering local Water User Societies for better asset management.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Sustainable farming by Irrigation Management Transfer (IMT) to Water User Society (WUS) for management of irrigation assets. WUS will receive handholding support for the next five years, helping them connect with economic entities like Farmer Producer Organizations (FPOs).
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Rewritten from candidate to improve conceptual clarity and align with UPSC's focus on institutional mechanisms in agriculture.

acceptedQuestions.subject: Economy
acceptedQuestions.prompt:
Regarding the Command Area Development and Water Management (CAD&WM) Programme, consider the following statements:
1. It was originally launched in the mid-1970s and was restructured and renamed in 2004.
2. Since 2015-16, it has been implemented under the 'Har Khet Ko Pani' component of the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY).
3. Adaptive trials and demonstrations regarding water use efficiency are classified as 'Structural' interventions under the programme.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The programme was launched in 1974-75 and renamed/restructured in 2004. Statement 2 is correct: It is currently implemented under the PMKSY-Har Khet Ko Pani component. Statement 3 is incorrect: Adaptive trials, demonstrations, and trainings are classified as 'Non-Structural' interventions. Structural interventions include On-Farm Development (OFD) works and construction of drains.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: CAD&WM distinguishes between physical infrastructure (structural) and capacity building/grants (non-structural).
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Genesis: It was launched in 1974-75 and was restructured and renamed as Command Area Development and Water Management (CADWM) Programme in 2004... It is being implemented under PMKSY - Har Khet Ko Pani from 2015-16... Non-Structural intervention: One time Functional Grant to the registered Water Users' Associations (WUAs); Trainings, demonstration, and adaptive trials with respect to water use efficiency.
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Tests the historical evolution and the structural/non-structural classification which is a common UPSC theme.

reviewerNotes: The questions cover the new M-CADWM sub-scheme, the historical context of CADWM, and the Mission for Aatmanirbharta in Pulses. The candidate question on IMT was rewritten to better reflect UPSC's conceptual style. All questions are strictly grounded in the provided text, distinguishing between structural/non-structural components and technical/institutional features.
